title: ajuga vulgaris subsp. genevensis
intro: Ajuga vulgaris subsp. genevensis, also known as Bugle, is a flowering plant species within the mint family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Ajuga vulgaris subsp. genevensis include its ability to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are commonly used in folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
